data.title: Microservices
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture, which breaks down applications into smaller, independent services.
data.technical_spec: Use of APIs and service-oriented architecture for deploying individual services.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: The architecture is designed to scale in response to varying customer demands.
data.technical_spec: Elastic Load Balancing, auto-scaling groups, and distributed databases.

data.title: High Availability
data.description: Ensures services are available and resilient to failures across multiple regions and zones.

data.title: Serverless Computing
data.description: Adoption of serverless paradigms to reduce resource allocation complexity.
data.technical_spec: Use of AWS Lambda and API Gateway for serverless functions.

data.title: Containerized Deployment
data.description: Efficient deployment and management of applications using container orchestration.
data.technical_spec: Use of Docker containers and Kubernetes.
